name: Newspapers and magazines
summary: Clean and dry paper can usually go into a paper recycling route in Hong Kong.
preparation_steps: Keep the paper dry and free from food residue.
Bundle or stack it neatly if your collection point asks for that.
Remove obvious plastic wrapping when practical.
drop_off_options: Paper stream at GREEN@COMMUNITY or another local recycling point.
Building or estate paper recycling arrangement, if provided.
if_no_route: If no paper recycling channel is available, follow your building's ordinary refuse arrangement.
note: Paper is one of the core recyclables commonly accepted by GREEN@COMMUNITY.
askPrompt: How should I recycle newspapers and magazines in Hong Kong?

name: Cardboard box
summary: Flattened cardboard boxes are usually suitable for paper recycling when they are reasonably clean.
preparation_steps: Empty the box completely.
Flatten it to save space.
Remove foam, plastic wrap, or large tape sections when practical.
drop_off_options: Paper stream at GREEN@COMMUNITY or a local recycling station.
Building paper recycling area, if available.
if_no_route: If the box is badly contaminated or no paper stream is available, treat it as general waste.
note: Cleaner cardboard is much easier for recyclers to handle.
askPrompt: Can I recycle a cardboard delivery box in Hong Kong?

name: Plastic beverage bottle
summary: Plastic beverage bottles can usually go into the plastics route after they are emptied and lightly rinsed.
preparation_steps: Empty the bottle fully.
Rinse out leftover drink if needed.
Separate the cap and label when practical.
drop_off_options: Plastics stream at GREEN@COMMUNITY.
Building plastics recycling point, if available.
not_accepted: Bottles heavily contaminated with food or chemicals
if_no_route: If there is no plastics collection channel nearby, keep the bottle dry and wait for a suitable collection point when possible.
note: Plastics are commonly accepted at community collection points, but cleaner items have a better chance of being recycled.
askPrompt: How should I recycle a plastic drink bottle in Hong Kong?

name: Bubble tea cup and lid
summary: Disposable drink cups are often mixed-material items and are usually treated as general waste unless every part is clearly clean and separately accepted.
preparation_steps: Pour out any leftover drink and pearls.
Separate obvious clean parts only if your local collection point clearly accepts them.
Do not mix food-stained cups into clean recycling streams.
drop_off_options: Ordinary refuse arrangement for most mixed or contaminated cups.
not_accepted: Cups still containing straws or food residue
if_no_route: Use ordinary refuse unless you can clearly separate clean recyclable parts that your local collection point accepts.
note: Mixed plastic, seal film, and drink residue make takeaway cups harder to recycle than simple bottles.
askPrompt: Where should I throw away a bubble tea cup in Hong Kong?

name: Clean takeaway plastic container
summary: A plastic takeaway container may go into the plastics route only if food residue and grease are removed well enough.
preparation_steps: Remove all food scraps first.
Quickly rinse off sauce or grease.
Separate non-plastic parts if practical.
drop_off_options: Plastics stream at GREEN@COMMUNITY or a similar plastics collection point.
if_no_route: If the container stays greasy or there is no accepted plastics route, place it in general waste.
note: Cleanliness matters more for food packaging than for bottles or cans.
askPrompt: Can I recycle a clean takeaway plastic container in Hong Kong?

name: Snack packet or instant noodle pouch
summary: Multi-layer snack packaging is usually not suitable for standard plastics recycling.
preparation_steps: Empty the packet fully.
Keep it out of normal plastics recycling.
drop_off_options: Ordinary refuse arrangement.
if_no_route: Treat this as general waste unless a specialist soft-plastic or composite-packaging programme is explicitly available.
note: Composite packaging is much harder to recycle than simple plastic bottles or containers.
askPrompt: Should I recycle a snack packet in Hong Kong?

name: Aluminium can
summary: A drink can usually fits the metals route once it is empty and lightly rinsed.
preparation_steps: Empty the can completely.
Rinse out leftover drink.
Keep it reasonably dry.
drop_off_options: Metals stream at GREEN@COMMUNITY or a local metals collection point.
if_no_route: Keep it dry and use the next available metals collection channel when possible.
note: Metals are one of the common community recycling streams in Hong Kong.
askPrompt: How should I recycle an aluminium can in Hong Kong?

name: Glass beverage bottle
summary: Container glass such as beverage bottles normally goes into the glass container route after emptying and rinsing.
preparation_steps: Empty the bottle fully.
Rinse out any leftover liquid.
Remove caps or pumps when practical.
drop_off_options: Glass bottle collection bins or a recognised community collection point.
if_no_route: If no glass container collection point is available nearby, follow your local refuse arrangement carefully and protect others from sharp edges.
note: Hong Kong glass collection routes focus on container glass rather than every glass-like item.
askPrompt: Where should I recycle a glass bottle in Hong Kong?

name: Mirror or broken ceramic mug
summary: Mirrors and ceramics should stay out of container-glass recycling and usually go with general waste.
preparation_steps: Wrap sharp edges safely before disposal.
Keep them out of bottle-glass recycling.
drop_off_options: Ordinary refuse arrangement.
if_no_route: Use ordinary refuse and wrap sharp pieces carefully.
note: Bottle glass programmes are intended for containers rather than mirrors or ceramics.
askPrompt: Can I recycle a mirror or broken ceramic mug in Hong Kong?

name: Milk or juice carton
summary: Beverage cartons usually need their own dedicated collection route instead of being mixed with ordinary paper.
preparation_steps: Empty the carton fully.
Rinse it, drain it, and flatten it.
Remove straws or extra wrapping when practical.
drop_off_options: Dedicated beverage carton stream at GREEN@COMMUNITY or a recognised collection partner.
not_accepted: Ordinary paper-only collection points that do not mention cartons
if_no_route: If you do not have access to a dedicated carton collection route, follow your local refuse arrangement.
note: Cartons are accepted by GREEN@COMMUNITY, but they should not be treated exactly like ordinary paper.
askPrompt: How should I recycle a milk or juice carton in Hong Kong?

name: Rechargeable battery or power bank
summary: Rechargeable batteries and power banks should go through a dedicated battery collection route.
preparation_steps: Do not place it in general recycling bins.
Tape exposed terminals when practical.
Keep damaged batteries separated and handled carefully.
drop_off_options: Rechargeable battery stream at GREEN@COMMUNITY or another recognised battery collection point.
not_accepted: Ordinary paper, plastics, metals, or glass streams
if_no_route: Store it safely until you can access a proper rechargeable battery collection point.
note: Rechargeable batteries are one of the common dedicated recycling categories in Hong Kong.
askPrompt: Where should I recycle a rechargeable battery or power bank in Hong Kong?

name: Disposable household battery
summary: Disposable household batteries are not handled the same way as rechargeable batteries in most community collection schemes.
preparation_steps: Keep them separate from rechargeable batteries.
Follow your ordinary refuse arrangement unless a dedicated local programme says otherwise.
drop_off_options: Ordinary refuse arrangement unless a specific local battery scheme accepts them.
not_accepted: Rechargeable battery collection points, unless they clearly accept disposable batteries
if_no_route: Do not assume every battery bin accepts disposable batteries; follow the local instruction at the collection point.
note: In Hong Kong, community routes commonly highlight rechargeable batteries specifically.
askPrompt: How should I dispose of a disposable household battery in Hong Kong?

name: Fluorescent tube or compact fluorescent lamp
summary: Fluorescent lamps and tubes should use a dedicated lamp collection route rather than normal bins.
preparation_steps: Keep the lamp unbroken if possible.
Handle it carefully and avoid mixing it with container glass.
Pack broken pieces safely if breakage happens.
drop_off_options: GREEN@COMMUNITY lamp collection or another recognised fluorescent-lamp collection route.
if_no_route: Keep it safe and intact until you can reach a suitable collection point.
note: Fluorescent lamps and tubes are accepted in dedicated collection channels because they need more careful handling.
askPrompt: Where should I bring a fluorescent tube in Hong Kong?

name: Household LED light bulb
summary: Household LED light bulbs can be taken to accepted community recycling channels instead of bottle-glass bins.
preparation_steps: Keep the bulb unbroken if possible.
Do not place it in glass bottle recycling.
Wrap it lightly if you need to carry it safely.
drop_off_options: GREEN@COMMUNITY collection where household LED bulbs are accepted.
if_no_route: If your local collection point does not accept it, follow the posted instruction or use ordinary refuse with safe wrapping.
note: LED bulbs are treated differently from glass bottles and should not go into container-glass recycling.
askPrompt: Can I recycle an LED light bulb in Hong Kong?

name: Small electrical appliance
summary: Small household appliances usually go through a dedicated small electrical appliance route.
preparation_steps: Unplug the appliance and keep it dry.
Bundle loose wires if practical.
Empty any water or food residue before drop-off.
drop_off_options: GREEN@COMMUNITY or another recognised small electrical appliance collection point.
not_accepted: Paper, plastics, metals, or general recycling bins
if_no_route: Keep the appliance until you can access a proper e-waste collection channel.
note: Small appliances have their own recycling route because they contain mixed materials and electronic parts.
askPrompt: How should I recycle a small electrical appliance in Hong Kong?

name: Computer, monitor, fridge, washer, or air conditioner
summary: Regulated electrical equipment should use seller take-back or an approved e-waste collection service.
preparation_steps: Do not place the item in ordinary recycling bins.
Arrange seller removal or an approved collection service when available.
Back up and erase data from computers or phones before handover if relevant.
drop_off_options: Seller take-back service.
Approved regulated e-waste collection service such as ALBA IWS.
if_no_route: Arrange an approved collection service instead of leaving the item in a refuse area.
note: Large and regulated electrical equipment in Hong Kong usually needs a dedicated removal arrangement.
askPrompt: How should I dispose of a computer or fridge in Hong Kong?

name: Food scraps and fruit peels
summary: Food waste should stay separate from dry recyclables and use a food-waste route when your building or district provides one.
preparation_steps: Drain extra liquid first when possible.
Keep food waste separate from clean recyclables.
Follow your building's food-waste container rules if one is provided.
drop_off_options: Building or estate food-waste bin, if available.
Food Waste Recycling Point or another official food-waste programme when available.
not_accepted: Dry recycling streams such as paper, plastics, metals, or glass
if_no_route: If your building has no food-waste collection, follow the ordinary refuse arrangement and avoid mixing food with recyclables.
note: Food-waste collection availability still varies by building and district in Hong Kong.
askPrompt: Where should I put food scraps in Hong Kong?

name: Used tissue paper and paper towels
summary: Used tissues and paper towels should normally stay out of clean paper recycling.
preparation_steps: Bag them if they are wet or messy.
Keep them out of clean paper recycling.
drop_off_options: Ordinary refuse arrangement.
note: Used tissue products are too contaminated for normal paper recycling.
askPrompt: Can I recycle used tissue paper in Hong Kong?
